assertions.title: Stop scan in progress

steps.action: Launched a fresh isolated fixture with zero cached discovered rows, opened `Re-scan`, and pressed `Start Scan` against a slowed-down filesystem tree containing an early `quick-project` skill and a delayed `zzzz-late-project` skill.
steps.expected: While the scan was active, the real user surface should expose a `Stop & Show Results` control so the user can stop the scan and keep only the partial results found so far.
steps.observed: No `Stop & Show Results` control or progress text ever became accessible on the real user surface during the scan attempt, even though the scan completed and discovered both projects.

steps.action: Inspected the isolated SQLite database after the scan attempt and captured the resulting Discover page.
steps.expected: Stopping early should leave only partial results (the early project) visible after the stop action.
steps.observed: The database contained both `quick-project` and `zzzz-late-project`, and the Discover page showed `2 skills across 2 projects`, confirming the scan ran to completion instead of exposing a working stop/partial-results flow.

assertions.issues: The active scan could not be interrupted from the real user surface; the stop control never became accessible before the full scan finished.

frictions.description: The Discover scan was run through the native Tauri window because the browser preview does not expose Tauri globals.
frictions.resolution: Used isolated native-window AX automation plus SQLite inspection to verify the stop-scan contract on the real user surface.

summary: VAL-DISC-007 failed in the isolated stop-scan fixture: starting a scan never surfaced an accessible `Stop & Show Results` control, and the scan completed with both projects discovered instead of allowing the user to stop early and keep partial results.
